title: 7.10 External Sort
subtitle: Run generation and multi-way merge on disk

content.introduction: External sort handles datasets larger than memory by generating sorted runs and merging them with disk-aware strategies.

sections.title: 7.10.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define run generation, multi-way merge, and I/O cost as primary optimization targets.
examples: Explain why in-memory sort is insufficient for huge files
Define run length based on memory budget
Compare two-way and multi-way merge passes

sections.title: 7.10.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe two-phase process: create sorted chunks in memory, then perform k-way merge with buffers.
examples: Generate sorted runs from chunked input
Merge runs with a min-heap of current heads
Track temporary file lifecycle during merge phases

sections.title: 7.10.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Discuss I/O complexity, buffer allocation, and practical tuning for throughput on storage systems.
examples: Estimate number of merge passes for N records
Tune k-way merge by available buffers
Use external sort in log processing and ETL pipelines

keyTakeaways: Manage run generation and multi-way merge
Optimize for I/O rather than CPU only
Plan buffer and temporary storage usage
Apply external sort to large-scale data processing
